name: @vanillagreen/pi-background-tasks
description: Pi extension for explicit non-blocking background shell tasks, log tails, and completion wakeups.

settings.label: Enable background tasks
settings.description: Register bg_task/bg_status, /bg, dashboard shortcut, and task widgets. Disable and reload to remove the feature.

settings.label: Glyph style
settings.description: Glyphs for vstack-owned UI chrome. Unicode is the default; ASCII uses plain box/status/separator characters for terminal compatibility. pi-tool-renderer's global override wins when set.

settings.label: Default timeout
settings.description: Default timeout for spawned background tasks. Default 0 disables timeouts so long-running work is not killed unexpectedly.

settings.label: Auto-background blocking bash monitors
settings.description: Automatically divert clearly long-running bash monitors (watch, tail -f, pi-bridge/tmux polling loops) into bg_task before they can block the turn.

settings.label: Extra auto-background patterns
settings.description: Optional newline-separated regular expressions for additional bash commands that should be auto-backgrounded before execution. Lines starting with # are ignored.

settings.label: Shortcut output wakeups
settings.description: When alt+. or /bg next forces the next bash into the background, also wake the agent on new output. Exit wakeups are always enabled for forced background tasks.

settings.label: Shortcut arming window
settings.description: Seconds after alt+. or /bg next during which the next bash command is forced into the background.

settings.label: Output settle delay
settings.description: Debounce delay before output wakeups are sent.

settings.label: Force-kill grace
settings.description: Milliseconds to wait after SIGTERM before SIGKILL.

settings.label: Resource controls
settings.description: Opt in to lower-priority execution for background tasks. Disabled by default; when off, spawn and stop behavior is unchanged.

settings.label: Resource control mode
settings.description: auto prefers a probed systemd-run --user transient service on Linux, then nice/ionice when available. off disables controls even if the master toggle is on.

settings.label: Apply controls to bg_task
settings.description: Apply resource controls to explicit bg_task and /bg:run spawns.

settings.label: Apply controls to auto-backgrounded bash
settings.description: Apply resource controls to bash commands diverted by auto-backgrounding, /bg next, or the arm-next-bash shortcut.

settings.label: CPU weight
settings.description: systemd-run CPUWeight value (1-10000). Lower values yield CPU to other work. Default 100 matches systemd's normal weight.

settings.label: I/O weight
settings.description: systemd-run IOWeight value (1-10000). Lower values yield I/O bandwidth on cgroup-v2 systems. Default 100 matches systemd's normal weight.

settings.label: Nice value
settings.description: CPU niceness for systemd-run Nice= and nice fallback (-20 to 19). Higher values are lower priority.

settings.label: ionice class
settings.description: I/O scheduling class for systemd-run IOSchedulingClass= and ionice fallback.

settings.label: ionice level
settings.description: I/O scheduling priority for realtime/best-effort classes (0 highest, 7 lowest).

settings.label: Warn on resource-control fallback
settings.description: Show at most one warning/diagnostic when controls fall back or no-op because helpers are unavailable.

settings.label: In-memory output buffer
settings.description: Maximum characters retained in memory per task; logs still keep full output.

settings.label: Wakeup output tail
settings.description: Characters included in output/exit wakeup messages. Wakes are custom steer messages that bypass pi-output-policy, so this is the per-wake transcript budget. Default 2000 keeps a single wake comfortably under 4KB; raise only for monitors whose verbose inline tail is genuinely useful.

settings.label: Dashboard/log tail
settings.description: Characters shown by dashboard and log actions. Log tool output includes the full log path when truncated.

settings.label: Output wake budget (count)
settings.description: Maximum output wakes per task before further output wakes are suppressed and a single 'wake budget exhausted; inspect log' notice is emitted. Exit wakes are unaffected. Set 0 to disable the count cap.

settings.label: Output wake budget (bytes)
settings.description: Cumulative inline output-tail bytes per task before further output wakes are suppressed. Set 0 to disable the byte cap.

settings.label: Show task widget
settings.description: Show the compact background-task widget while tasks are tracked. Manual hide remains latched across lifecycle refreshes until explicit toggle-in.

settings.label: Widget placement
settings.description: Where the compact widget appears.

settings.label: Tool output style
settings.description: Compact keeps bg_task results to one status line; stacked adds short task/log rows and ctrl+o to expand details.

settings.label: Expanded tool log lines
settings.description: Maximum log lines shown after ctrl+o to expand bg_task log output.

settings.label: Dashboard output line cap
settings.description: Maximum output lines kept in the interactive dashboard viewport. Full logs remain on disk.

settings.label: Background next bash shortcut
settings.description: Keyboard shortcut that arms a one-shot diversion: the next not-yet-started bash command becomes a bg_task. Already-running bash cannot be detached safely. Use "none" to disable. Requires restart because shortcuts register at extension load.

settings.label: Mini-dashboard toggle shortcut
settings.description: Keyboard shortcut to show/hide the inline mini-dashboard and restore the last visible mode when toggled back in. Requires restart because shortcuts register at extension load.

settings.label: Mini-dashboard default mode
settings.description: Initial inline mini-dashboard mode at session start; runtime hide/show state is controlled by the toggle.

settings.label: Mini-dashboard finished retention
settings.description: Seconds to keep finished tasks visible in the inline mini-dashboard. Full dashboard entries persist until session end or manual clear.

settings.label: Dashboard shortcut
settings.description: Keyboard shortcut registered for the dashboard. Requires restart because shortcuts register at extension load.

settings.label: Task log directory
settings.description: Directory for task log files. PI_BG_TASK_DIR still overrides this setting.

author: vanillagreen
